prompt: Psychological first aid after a traumatic workplace event should include:

options.text: Mandatory public recounting of trauma without support
Practical support, signposting to professional help, and avoiding forced disclosure or blame
Immediate dismissal of all witnesses
Promising that similar events cannot recur without investigation

explanation: Post-incident support emphasises safety, practical help, and access to specialists — not coerced debrief or punitive responses.

distractorExplanations.c: Dismissal does not address psychosocial recovery needs.
distractorExplanations.d: Investigation and learning should follow, not empty promises.

prompt: A risk assessment for a new seated assembly task identifies static neck flexion for seven hours. The best control package is:

options.text: Higher production targets without ergonomic change
Standing-only work with no anti-fatigue mat
Adjustable bench and chair, task variation, micro-breaks, and tool balancers to reduce reach
Removal of all job rotation indefinitely

explanation: Static posture MSDs need workstation fit, variation, and engineering aids — not just production pressure.

distractorExplanations.a: Targets without ergonomic change sustain risk.
distractorExplanations.b: Standing all day introduces different MSD risks without design.
distractorExplanations.d: Rotation can help when combined with redesign.

prompt: Which welfare issue on a refurbishment site most directly increases physical health risk?

options.text: Too many fire extinguishers
Excess directional signage
Over-provision of shaded rest areas
Inadequate washing facilities after contact with cement-based products

explanation: Wet cement can cause dermatitis; adequate washing helps remove irritants and supports skin health.

distractorExplanations.a: Fire extinguishers support safety, not welfare deficiency.
distractorExplanations.b: Fire extinguishers support safety, not welfare deficiency.
distractorExplanations.c: Signage excess is not a typical health risk.

prompt: When assessing individual capability for manual handling, which factor is most relevant?

options.text: Existing injury, pregnancy, training, and strength relative to the task demands
Favourite sports team of the worker
Vehicle colour they drive to work
Number of years until retirement alone

explanation: Individual assessment considers health, capability, experience, and special circumstances affecting safe handling.

distractorExplanations.b: Personal preferences unrelated to capacity are irrelevant.
distractorExplanations.c: Commute details do not determine handling ability.
distractorExplanations.d: Retirement timeline alone is not a capability measure.

prompt: An organisation wants to reduce sedentary behaviour among office staff. The most evidence-aligned approach is:

options.text: Ban all movement during core hours
Encourage standing breaks, walking meetings where practical, and adjustable sit-stand desks
Remove chairs to force kneeling
Measure steps only without changing work design

explanation: Physical activity at work is supported by breaks, adjustable furniture, and culture — not punitive removal of seating.

prompt: Hand-arm vibration controls should prioritise:

options.text: Using the highest vibration tool for shorter total career length
Disabling tool isolators to speed work
Selecting lower-vibration tools, maintaining equipment, limiting trigger time, and anti-vibration gloves only as supplementary
Relying solely on cotton gloves during winter

explanation: The hierarchy favours lower-vibration equipment, maintenance, and work organisation before PPE.

distractorExplanations.a: Career length is not a substitute for exposure control.
distractorExplanations.b: Disabled isolators can increase vibration transmission.
distractorExplanations.d: Cotton gloves do not replace anti-vibration or limit exposure time.

prompt: A worker cleaning with strong disinfectants develops respiratory irritation. Besides PPE, which health measure is appropriate?

options.text: Increase concentration to finish faster
Mix disinfectants to improve fragrance
Ignore SDS because labels are optional
Review SDS, ventilation, product substitution, and health surveillance if exposure warrants

explanation: Chemical health risks need SDS-based controls, ventilation, substitution, and surveillance per COSHH principles.

distractorExplanations.b: Mixing chemicals can create dangerous reactions.
distractorExplanations.c: Mixing chemicals can create dangerous reactions.

prompt: Early reporting of musculoskeletal discomfort is valuable because:

options.text: It allows ergonomic intervention before reversible strain becomes chronic injury
It automatically triggers disciplinary action
It removes the need for any risk assessment
It guarantees compensation without investigation

explanation: Early intervention culture prevents escalation to lost-time injury and supports suitable adjustments.

distractorExplanations.b: Reporting should be encouraged, not punished.
distractorExplanations.d: Compensation processes are separate from prevention.

prompt: For thermal comfort in an office, which adjustment is most effective?

options.text: Lock thermostat at 30°C year-round
Manage HVAC setpoints, airflow, humidity, and allow local layering within dress code
Prohibit all personal fans and heaters without assessment
Ignore complaints because comfort is subjective only

explanation: Thermal comfort balances environmental control with reasonable local adjustments; persistent issues warrant investigation.

distractorExplanations.a: Excessive heat causes fatigue and discomfort.
distractorExplanations.c: Blanket bans may ignore reasonable needs.
distractorExplanations.d: Subjective comfort still signals potential health and performance issues.

prompt: A driver exposed to WBV complains of back pain. The assessment should examine:

options.text: Only the driver's domestic mattress brand
Screen refresh rate in the depot office
Seat suspension, road conditions, driving hours, maintenance, and posture
Canteen menu calorie counts exclusively

explanation: WBV assessment covers vehicle seat, route, duration, and maintenance — key syllabus content for vibration health.

distractorExplanations.a: Domestic factors are secondary to occupational exposure.
distractorExplanations.b: Domestic factors are secondary to occupational exposure.
distractorExplanations.d: Nutrition alone does not assess WBV.

prompt: Which statement about workplace bullying and mental health is most accurate?

options.text: Bullying improves resilience and should be tolerated
Mental health is never work-related
Only physical injuries count in OH&S law
Sustained bullying is a psychosocial hazard that can cause anxiety, depression, and absence

explanation: Psychosocial hazards including bullying are recognised OH&S issues requiring prevention and intervention.

distractorExplanations.a: Bullying harms health and culture; it must not be normalised.
distractorExplanations.b: Work can contribute significantly to mental ill health.
distractorExplanations.c: Work can contribute significantly to mental ill health.

prompt: Signage stating 'Think before you lift' in a storeroom with only floor-level pallets is weak because:

options.text: It relies on administrative control without addressing poor storage height and task design
Posters are always illegal on warehouse walls
Floor-level storage is always prohibited by law
Workers cannot read signage in warehouses

explanation: Effective control redesigns storage and handling; slogans alone leave biomechanical hazards unchanged.

distractorExplanations.b: Posters can supplement but not replace engineering controls.
distractorExplanations.c: Floor storage is common; risk must be managed, not assumed banned.
distractorExplanations.d: Literacy and visibility are separate from control adequacy.

prompt: Health surveillance for noise-exposed workers typically includes:

options.text: Annual vision tests only
Baseline and periodic audiometry with record keeping and follow-up on standard threshold shifts
Random drug testing unrelated to hearing
Fitness to drive tests for all office staff

explanation: Audiometry detects early hearing loss in noise programmes, triggering review of exposure and protection.

distractorExplanations.a: Vision tests do not monitor NIHL.
distractorExplanations.c: Drug testing is unrelated unless part of separate policy.
distractorExplanations.d: Office staff not in noise roles do not need audiometry for noise.

prompt: A manual handling operation can be avoided entirely by:

options.text: Asking workers to lift faster in pairs without equipment
Reducing packaging size while keeping floor-level pickup
Installing conveyors, chutes, or mechanical lifts to move materials without human lifting
Issuing wrist braces for all staff

explanation: Elimination/substitution at the top of the hierarchy removes or reduces handling through mechanisation.

distractorExplanations.a: Faster lifting increases risk without redesign.
distractorExplanations.b: Smaller loads may still require hazardous postures from floor level.
distractorExplanations.d: Braces do not eliminate handling hazard.

prompt: Night workers' health is best protected by:

options.text: Bright blue-rich lighting only with no rest policy
Eliminating all meal breaks to increase output
Scheduling hardest manual tasks at end of 12-hour night shift
Fatigue risk assessment, controlled overtime, suitable lighting, and access to welfare facilities on shift

explanation: Shift design, lighting, nutrition breaks, and fatigue management support physical and mental health at night.

distractorExplanations.a: Lighting alone without fatigue policy is incomplete.
distractorExplanations.b: Removing meals harms health and performance.
distractorExplanations.c: Removing meals harms health and performance.

prompt: An ergonomic risk assessment of a lab microscope station should consider:

options.text: Eyepiece height, seat support, forearm position, and break frequency from static posture
Only the chemical inventory in adjacent cupboards
Parking allocation for visitors
Brand of lab coats exclusively

explanation: Microscope work creates neck and upper limb static load; DSE/ergonomic principles apply to lab tasks.

distractorExplanations.b: Chemical inventory is COSHH-related, not primary for microscope posture.
distractorExplanations.d: Lab coat brand does not determine ergonomic setup.

prompt: Workers in a cold store report numb hands and reduced dexterity. Besides thermal PPE, what should be assessed?

options.text: Increasing exposure time to build tolerance without limits
Work-rest cycles, buddy systems, warm-up breaks, and manual handling in cold conditions
Removing gloves to improve grip on metal at -20°C
Ignoring dexterity loss because cold is temporary

explanation: Cold environments affect dexterity and MSD risk; work organisation and PPE together manage physical health.

distractorExplanations.a: Unlimited cold exposure risks frostbite and errors.
distractorExplanations.c: Bare-hand contact with cold metal is hazardous.
distractorExplanations.d: Dexterity loss increases accident and handling risk.

prompt: Which factor most strongly links poor welfare to increased accident rates?

options.text: Excess availability of drinking water
Too frequent legally compliant breaks
Fatigue and dehydration from inadequate rest, hydration, and sanitary facilities reducing concentration
Clear signage to welfare units

explanation: Poor welfare undermines alertness and wellbeing, indirectly raising accident likelihood alongside direct health harm.

prompt: A company measures psychosocial health using confidential staff surveys. Results show low role clarity and high conflict. Next step:

options.text: Publish named individual survey answers in the canteen
Ignore results because they are not injury statistics
Increase conflict by removing team leaders
Develop action plans with worker input to clarify roles and address conflict sources

explanation: Survey findings should drive targeted organisational interventions with confidentiality and participation.

distractorExplanations.a: Named publication breaches confidentiality and worsens conflict.
distractorExplanations.b: Psychosocial data is valid OH&S intelligence.
distractorExplanations.c: Psychosocial data is valid OH&S intelligence.

prompt: When selecting hearing protection, the most important fit criterion is:

options.text: Appropriate SNR/attenuation for the noise level with correct fit and compatibility with other PPE
Brightest colour regardless of attenuation
Smallest size for all workers without fit test
Reuse of damaged ear plugs indefinitely

explanation: Protection must reduce exposure adequately while fitting the user and not conflicting with helmets or communication needs.

distractorExplanations.b: Colour does not indicate protection level.
distractorExplanations.c: One size without fit check reduces effectiveness.

prompt: A warehouse introduces palletisers but keeps manual handling for awkward items. The risk assessment should:

options.text: Be deleted because automation removed all risk
Be updated to cover remaining manual tasks, training on exceptions, and maintenance of new equipment
Ignore awkward items as too rare to document
Remove training because machines are automatic

explanation: Partial automation leaves residual manual handling risks requiring updated assessment and competence.

distractorExplanations.a: Residual manual tasks still pose MSD risk.
distractorExplanations.c: Rare tasks still need control if foreseeable.
distractorExplanations.d: Training covers exceptions and safe interaction with machinery.

prompt: Occupational dermatitis from wet work is best prevented by:

options.text: Requiring longer wet exposure to harden skin
Banning all moisturiser on site
Substitution of harsh agents, gloves where suitable, skin care programme, and drying facilities
Using solvents to clean hands without washing facilities

explanation: Skin health programmes combine hygiene, suitable gloves, gentler products, and welfare washing/drying.

distractorExplanations.a: Prolonged wet work increases dermatitis risk.
distractorExplanations.b: Moisturiser can be part of skin care when policy allows.
distractorExplanations.d: Solvents harm skin and require proper washing.

prompt: An employee working at dual monitors reports neck rotation pain. The ergonomic fix is:

options.text: Place both screens far to one side
Lower chairs so screens are below knee height
Increase font size only without moving screens
Position primary screen directly ahead and angle secondary monitor to minimise sustained neck twist

explanation: Monitor placement should limit sustained asymmetric neck posture — core DSE competency for IGC.

prompt: Vibration exposure time for a worker using two different tools in one shift should be calculated by:

options.text: Combining partial exposures using trigger time and manufacturer vibration data against limit values
Ignoring the second tool if it is lighter colour
Averaging only the loudest noise readings
Assuming one minute of use equals zero exposure

explanation: Multiple tool exposures are summed in A(8) terms; syllabus expects understanding of exposure addition.

distractorExplanations.b: Noise readings do not substitute for vibration data.
distractorExplanations.d: All trigger time contributes to daily exposure.

prompt: Senior management support for ergonomic improvements is important because:

options.text: Ergonomics is only a worker hobby
Resource allocation and culture determine whether assessment findings become sustained controls
MSDs resolve without management action
Purchasing cheaper non-adjustable furniture always suffices

explanation: Without management commitment, ergonomic assessments stall at paperwork; culture and budget enable redesign.

distractorExplanations.a: Ergonomics is a recognised OH&S discipline.
distractorExplanations.d: Non-adjustable furniture may perpetuate poor fit.
